title: Poonch Fort
tagline: Poonch Fort — heritage city of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Poonch Fort (known locally as Poonch Qila) is a historic fort and palace complex located in Poonch, in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
overview.description: Poonch Fort (known locally as Poonch Qila) is a historic fort and palace complex located in Poonch, in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The fort is one of the most significant architectural remnants of the former semi autonomous jagir of Poonch and reflects a combination of Mughal, Sikh, Dogra, and European architectural influences.

topPlaces.name: Poonch (town)
topPlaces.description: Poonch (or Punch), (called Prunts in the Kashmiri, Gojri & Pahari languages) is a city and the administrative headquarters of the Poonch district, of the Indian-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, which is part of the larger disputed territory of Kashmir. It is located near the Line of Control – the de facto border in the disputed region.

topPlaces.name: Kumar, Jammu and Kashmir

topPlaces.name: Mangnar
topPlaces.description: Mangnar is a village in Poonch district, India, Jammu and Kashmir, India. The name is a combination of two words 'mang' means plain and 'nar' means mountain.

items.note: Poonch (or Punch), (called Prunts in the Kashmiri, Gojri & Pahari languages) is a city and the administrative headquarters of the Poonch district, of the Indian-administered union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, which is part of the larger disputed territory of Kashmir. It is located near the Line of Control – the de facto border in the disputed region.
Kumār is a city located in Poonch, Jammu and Kashmir, India.
Mangnar is a village in Poonch district, India, Jammu and Kashmir, India. The name is a combination of two words 'mang' means plain and 'nar' means mountain.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Poonch Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Poonch Fort is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Poonch Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (118 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Poonch Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Poonch Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Poonch Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Poonch Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Poonch Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Poonch (town), Kumar, Jammu and Kashmir, Mangnar.
